reasoning: The combined statistical estimate from the FRED tool for Nov 30 is 0.59, and short-term momentum has been positive over the past month. Recent news (late Oct/early Nov) confirms the Fed intends to end Quantitative Tightening (QT) on December 1, 2025, which removes the structural downward pressure on reserve balances that has persisted over the last few years. While there could be downward pressure from TGA rebuilding (targeting $850B later in the quarter) and mid-December tax receipts, the end of runoff acts as a stabilizing floor. Per the explicit instruction, the probabilities are pulled increasingly toward 0.5 for the more distant forecast horizons to reflect growing uncertainty over exactly how standard seasonal and operational fluctuations will net out against a static or potentially eventually expanding balance sheet in early 2026.
